adjudication_raw: All three assessors independently identify the candidate pool confound (different pools: 200 vs 500 candidates) as the dominant structural flaw. All three conclude the 4.5% NDCG@20 cannot be attributed to the ranking model without a factorial ablation. Assessor 1 and 2 also flag replay evaluation missing-data bias. Assessor 3 explicitly states the three compounding factors (retriever quality, pool size, reranker quality) are all folded into one number. Consensus: critique_wins. The issues are genuine but empirically resolvable by the 2x2 factorial.
all_issues_raised: Candidate set asymmetry confounds reranker comparison
Candidate pool size (200 vs 500) creates structural NDCG ceiling difference
No factorial ablation to separate retriever from reranker contribution
Replay evaluation missing-data bias against novel candidates
Leave-last-week-out and 5% sample valid but noted as minor secondary concerns

empirical_test.condition: Evaluate both rerankers (GBM and transformer) on the same frozen candidate pool (e.g., two-tower 500-item pool), measuring NDCG@20 per reranker independently
empirical_test.supports_critique_if: Transformer reranker shows less than 1% relative NDCG@20 gain over GBM when both see identical candidate inputs, or no gain
empirical_test.supports_defense_if: Transformer reranker shows at least 2% relative NDCG@20 gain over GBM on the same candidate pool
empirical_test.ambiguous_if: Transformer shows small positive gain (1-2%) on common pool but the gain varies substantially across user segments or time windows
